user_story: As a user, I want to set up multi‑factor authentication for my account, so that my personal data and access are protected against unauthorized logins.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a user, I want to securely log in using two‑factor authentication, so that my account is protected against unauthorized access.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a user, I want to log in using multi‑factor authentication, so that my account is protected from unauthorized access and I can trust the security of my personal data.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a user, I want to log in using multi‑factor authentication, so that my account is protected against unauthorized access and I can trust that only I can access my personal data.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a product manager, I want CRUD functionality for product listings, so that I can add new products, view existing ones, update details, and remove discontinued items.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a content editor, I want to create, read, update, and delete articles through the CMS, so that I can keep the website content fresh and accurate.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a content author, I want to be able to choose between saving my work as a draft or publishing it immediately, so that I can control when my content goes live and avoid accidental publication.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a content author, I want to be able to save my work as a draft and publish it separately, so that I can review and edit before making it public.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a content author, I want to be able to choose between saving my work as a draft or publishing it immediately, so that I can control the timing and quality of my content.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a customer, I want to search, browse, and filter products by multiple criteria (such as category, price range, brand, and rating), so that I can quickly find items that match my preferences and make informed purchase decisions.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a shopper, I want to search for products, browse categories, and apply filters (price, brand, rating, etc.) so that I can quickly locate items that match my specific needs and preferences.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a customer, I want to search for products, browse categories, and filter results by attributes such as price, brand, and rating, so that I can quickly find the items that best meet my needs.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a user, I want to search for items, browse categories, and filter results by price, brand, and rating, so that I can quickly find and compare products that meet my needs.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a developer, I want to add support for Duke Core’s core modules and API into our application, so that we can leverage Duke Core’s core functionalities, improve performance, and ensure seamless compatibility with future updates.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a support engineer, I want to implement automated health checks and alerting for Duke Core, so that we can detect failures early and reduce downtime.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a developer, I want to add support for Duke Core’s core library and APIs, so that our application can authenticate users with Duke credentials and seamlessly consume Duke Core data services.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a system administrator, I want to integrate Duke Core into our platform, so that we can leverage its core functionalities for improved data processing, reliability, and scalability.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create, edit, and link authority records for names, subjects, and corporate entities, so that the library catalog maintains consistent, disambiguated entries and improves search accuracy for patrons.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want an authority control module that automatically suggests standardized author names and merges duplicate entries, so that my catalog remains accurate and users can reliably find all works by a given author.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the system to automatically link every author name entered to a unique authority record, so that the catalog remains consistent and users can reliably find all works by that author.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a content editor, I want a dynamic form that lets me create or update product listings, so that I can manage inventory efficiently and keep the catalog up‑to‑date.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a product manager, I want to create and update the interface for the product catalog, so that I can add new products and update existing ones without involving the development team.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a reusable create/update interface component, so that I can quickly implement forms for different entities with consistent validation, UX and minimal boilerplate.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As an inventory manager, I want to view and update the status of each item in the inventory system, so that I can maintain accurate stock levels and ensure timely restocking.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a warehouse manager, I want to view and update the status of items in the inventory system, so that I can ensure accurate stock levels and timely order fulfillment.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As a developer, I want the application to support UTF‑8 encoding for all text inputs, outputs, and database fields, so that I can reliably store, retrieve, and display international characters, emojis, and symbols without data loss or corruption.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to ingest metadata from our data sources into a central metadata repository, so that we can provide accurate data lineage and improve data discovery for analysts.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a data engineer, I want to ingest metadata from our data sources into a central metadata repository, so that we can provide accurate data cataloging, lineage, and governance for all downstream analytics and data science teams.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a digital librarian, I want to integrate our digital collections with external scholarly databases and institutional repositories, so that patrons can discover related content seamlessly and access all resources from a single interface.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a research librarian, I want to integrate our digital collections with external scholarly databases and institutional repositories, so that patrons can discover related primary sources and secondary literature in a single search interface.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a digital collections manager, I want to integrate our digital collections with external resources such as institutional repositories, scholarly databases, and multimedia archives, so that users can seamlessly discover and access related content across platforms.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a library of reusable, well‑documented UI components, so that I can quickly assemble pages with consistent styling and behavior, reducing development time and ensuring maintainability.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a library of well‑documented, reusable UI components, so that I can accelerate development and maintain visual consistency across the application.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a reusable component library with clear documentation and theming support, so that I can rapidly prototype and maintain consistent UI across multiple projects.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a library of reusable UI components, so that I can assemble pages quickly and maintain visual consistency across the application.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a library of reusable UI components, so that I can speed up development and ensure consistency across the application.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a well‑documented, reusable component library that can be easily imported and customized, so that I can build consistent user interfaces across multiple projects with minimal duplication and faster delivery.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a well‑documented, reusable component library that follows our design system, so that I can quickly build consistent, high‑quality UIs across multiple projects without reinventing the wheel.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a component library that includes reusable UI components with clear documentation and unit tests, so that I can build features faster and maintain consistency across the application.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a reusable component library, so that I can accelerate development and maintain UI consistency across the product.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a component library with reusable UI components, so that I can speed up development and maintain visual consistency across the application.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As a front‑end developer, I want a library of reusable UI components, so that I can build consistent interfaces quickly and maintain code quality.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to add, edit, and delete items in the collection database, so that I can keep the inventory accurate and up-to-date for researchers and visitors.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a museum curator, I want to manage a digital catalog of the collection—adding, editing, and tagging items with detailed metadata—so that I can maintain accurate records, streamline loan requests, and provide researchers with easy access to up‑to‑date information.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a museum curator, I want a digital collection management system that lets me add, edit, and track artifacts with detailed metadata, so that I can maintain an accurate inventory and provide researchers with reliable information.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to add, edit, and delete items in the collection database, so that I can keep the inventory accurate and up-to-date.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a collection manager, I want a digital inventory system that tracks each item’s location, condition, and loan status, so that I can efficiently manage the collection, reduce loss, and provide accurate data for reporting.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to generate automated reports on collection usage and condition, so that I can make data‑driven decisions about acquisitions and conservation priorities.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to create, edit, and delete metadata application profiles, so that I can maintain consistent metadata definitions across all applications and support data governance initiatives.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a Data Steward, I want to create and maintain metadata application profiles for each application, so that we can enforce consistent metadata standards and improve data quality across the organization.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to create and manage reusable metadata application profiles—defining schema, validation rules, and mapping templates—that can be applied to any application, so that developers can quickly enforce consistent metadata standards and ensure compliance with our data governance policies.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a Data Steward, I want to create, edit, and maintain metadata application profiles for each application, so that data governance teams can enforce consistent data definitions and integration teams can map data accurately across systems.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
